## Verification

### Automated Checks

- [ ] `pnpm turbo run lint` passes (no linter errors)
- [ ] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` passes (no TypeScript errors)
- [ ] `pnpm turbo run test` passes (all tests including new ones)
- [ ] `pnpm turbo run build` succeeds (all apps compile)
- [ ] CI pipeline passes (GitHub Actions)

### Manual Test Steps

> Provide step-by-step instructions for reviewers to verify the feature

1. **Setup**: [e.g., "Start API: `pnpm --filter @notiving/api dev`"]
2. **Step 1**: [e.g., "Start frontend: `pnpm --filter h5 dev`"]
3. **Step 2**: [e.g., "Navigate to http://localhost:5173/posts"]
4. **Step 3**: [e.g., "Login as test user (email: test@example.com, password: test123)"]
5. **Step 4**: [e.g., "Click heart icon on first post"]
6. **Expected Result**: [e.g., "Heart fills with red color, count increments by 1"]
7. **Step 5**: [e.g., "Click heart again"]
8. **Expected Result**: [e.g., "Heart empties, count decrements by 1"]

### Edge Cases Verified

- [ ] **Edge Case 1**: [e.g., "Unauthenticated user attempts to like → 401 error, login prompt shown"]
- [ ] **Edge Case 2**: [e.g., "Network error during like → error toast shown, retry button available"]
- [ ] **Edge Case 3**: [e.g., "Rapid clicking like button → debounced, no duplicate requests"]

## Quality Gates

> All items must be checked before requesting review

- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors (`tsc --noEmit`)
- [ ] All tests pass, including new tests added for this feature
- [ ] Code follows project conventions (`.claude/rules/coding.md`)
- [ ] No decrease in test coverage (check CI output)
- [ ] Conventional Commits format used (e.g., `feat(api): add post like endpoint`)
- [ ] No scope creep - only features in acceptance criteria are included
- [ ] All files in "Files Changed" section of task template are updated

## Reviewer Checklist

> For reviewers - verify these before approving

- [ ] PR matches task template structure (input/output clearly defined)
- [ ] All acceptance criteria are met and verification method is clear
- [ ] Manual test steps are provided and work as described
- [ ] Edge cases are covered (tests or manual verification)
- [ ] Code quality meets project standards (no `any`, proper error handling)
- [ ] Tests are comprehensive (happy path + error cases)
- [ ] No unnecessary changes outside the defined scope
- [ ] Documentation is updated where needed
